    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/setting/setting.go", "symbol": "applyCommandLineDefaultProperties"},

    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/setting/setting.go", "symbol": "parseINIFile"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/setting/provider.go", "symbol": "Current"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/setting/setting.go", "symbol": "RedactedValue"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/api/admin.go", "symbol": "AdminGetSettings"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/api/admin.go", "symbol": "getAuthorizedSettings"}
  ],
  "text": "The Grafana configuration loading system is implemented across several key files:\n\n**INI File Parsing** (`pkg/setting/setting.go`):\n- `loadConfiguration()` orchestrates the full config load: it reads `conf/defaults.ini` as the master file, then layers overrides.\n- `loadSpecifiedConfigFile()` loads a user-specified INI config file (e.g., `conf/custom.ini`) and merges its sections/keys into the master defaults file.\n- `NewCfgFromBytes()` and `NewCfgFromINIFile()` provide specialized constructors to create a `Cfg` from raw bytes or an already-parsed `ini.File`.\n- `parseINIFile()` takes the merged `ini.File` and populates all fields of the `Cfg` struct, calling section-specific read helpers like `readServerSettings()`, `readSecuritySettings()`, `readAuthSettings()`, etc.\n- `valueAsString()` is a helper to extract string values from ini sections with defaults.\n\n**Environment Variable Override Logic** (`pkg/setting/setting.go`):\n- `applyEnvVariableOverrides()` iterates all sections and keys of the merged INI file. For each key, it computes the env var name via `EnvKey()` (e.g., section `database`, key `password` becomes `GF_DATABASE_PASSWORD`) and, if that env var is set, overwrites the key value.\n- `EnvKey()` computes the canonical `GF_<SECTION>_<KEY>` environment variable name.\n- `SectionWithEnvOverrides()` / `DynamicSection` provides a runtime-override wrapper for sections that re-reads env vars live.\n\n**Command-Line Overrides** (`pkg/setting/setting.go`):\n- `applyCommandLineDefaultProperties()` applies `default.<section>.<key>` CLI properties to the defaults file before user config is merged.\n- `applyCommandLineProperties()` applies `<section>.<key>` CLI properties after user config and env overrides.\n\n**Config Value Expansion** (`pkg/setting/expanders.go`):\n- `expandConfig()` iterates all sections/keys and runs each registered `Expander` over values, supporting `${}` (env var interpolation) and `$__file{}` (file contents) syntax.\n- `envExpander` resolves `${VAR}` patterns from environment variables.\n- `fileExpander` resolves `$__file{/path}` patterns from file contents.\n\n**Default Values** (`conf/defaults.ini`):\n- The INI file containing all default configuration values per section, loaded as the base before any user config or env override is applied.\n\n**Sensitive Config Value Masking** (`pkg/setting/setting.go`, `pkg/setting/provider.go`, `pkg/api/admin.go`):\n- `RedactedValue()` (`setting.go`) checks if a key name matches sensitive patterns (PASSWORD, SECRET, PRIVATE_KEY, TOKEN, CLIENT_SECRET, etc.) using regex, returning `*********` for matches. It also calls `RedactedURL()` to strip credentials from URL values.\n- `RedactedURL()` uses `url.Parse()` and `url.Redacted()` to strip passwords from connection URLs.\n- `OSSImpl.Current()` (`provider.go`) calls `RedactedValue()` for every key when producing the `SettingsBag` snapshot returned to the API layer.\n- `AdminGetSettings()` and `getAuthorizedSettings()` (`api/admin.go`) serve the `/api/admin/settings` endpoint, returning the already-redacted `SettingsBag` filtered by RBAC access control permissions."
}

conversation_events.text:
# Grafana Configuration File Parsing and Override Chain

## Your Task

Find all Go source files in grafana/grafana that implement configuration loading: the INI file parser, the environment variable override logic, the default values per section, and the sensitive config value masking for the /api/admin/settings endpoint.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the platform domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec.


## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile the answer:

The answer has been written to `/workspace/answer.json`. Here's a summary of my findings:

## Grafana Configuration Loading Chain

### Core Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `pkg/setting/setting.go` | Main config orchestration, INI parsing, env overrides, sensitive value masking |
| `pkg/setting/provider.go` | `Provider` interface + `OSSImpl` which applies `RedactedValue` when serving settings |
| `pkg/setting/expanders.go` | `${}` and `$__file{}` value expansion in config keys |
| `pkg/api/admin.go` | `/api/admin/settings` endpoint handler |
| `conf/defaults.ini` | All default configuration values per section |

### Override Chain (in order)
1. **`conf/defaults.ini`** loaded as master file
2. **`applyCommandLineDefaultProperties()`** — `default.<section>.<key>` CLI flags
3. **`loadSpecifiedConfigFile()`** — user's custom INI file merged over defaults
4. **`applyEnvVariableOverrides()`** — `GF_<SECTION>_<KEY>` env vars (via `EnvKey()`)
5. **`applyCommandLineProperties()`** — `<section>.<key>` CLI flags
6. **`expandConfig()`** — `${}` / `$__file{}` value interpolation
7. **`parseINIFile()`** — populates `Cfg` struct from merged file

### Sensitive Value Masking
- `RedactedValue()` in `setting.go` matches key names against patterns (PASSWORD, SECRET, TOKEN, PRIVATE_KEY, etc.) and returns `*********`
- `OSSImpl.Current()` in `provider.go` calls `RedactedValue()` for every key when building the settings snapshot
- `AdminGetSettings()` in `api/admin.go` serves this already-redacted bag through RBAC filtering

    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/setting/provider.go", "symbol": "Provider"},

    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/setting/provider.go", "symbol": "ProvideProvider"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/setting/provider.go", "symbol": "SettingsBag"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/setting/provider.go", "symbol": "VerboseSettingsBag"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/api/admin.go", "symbol": "AdminGetSettings"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/api/admin.go", "symbol": "AdminGetVerboseSettings"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/api/admin.go", "symbol": "getAuthorizedSettings"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/api/admin.go", "symbol": "getAuthorizedVerboseSettings"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/setting/setting.go", "symbol": "Load"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/setting/setting.go", "symbol": "loadConfiguration"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/setting/setting.go", "symbol": "applyCommandLineDefaultProperties"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/setting/setting.go", "symbol": "loadSpecifiedConfigFile"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/setting/setting.go", "symbol": "applyEnvVariableOverrides"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/setting/setting.go", "symbol": "applyCommandLineProperties"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/setting/expanders.go", "symbol": "expandConfig"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/setting/setting.go", "symbol": "parseINIFile"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/setting/provider.go", "symbol": "Current"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/setting/setting.go", "symbol": "RedactedValue"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/api/admin.go", "symbol": "AdminGetSettings"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/api/admin.go", "symbol": "getAuthorizedSettings"}
  ],
  "text": "The Grafana configuration loading system is implemented across several key files:\n\n**INI File Parsing** (`pkg/setting/setting.go`):\n- `loadConfiguration()` orchestrates the full config load: it reads `conf/defaults.ini` as the master file, then layers overrides.\n- `loadSpecifiedConfigFile()` loads a user-specified INI config file (e.g., `conf/custom.ini`) and merges its sections/keys into the master defaults file.\n- `NewCfgFromBytes()` and `NewCfgFromINIFile()` provide specialized constructors to create a `Cfg` from raw bytes or an already-parsed `ini.File`.\n- `parseINIFile()` takes the merged `ini.File` and populates all fields of the `Cfg` struct, calling section-specific read helpers like `readServerSettings()`, `readSecuritySettings()`, `readAuthSettings()`, etc.\n- `valueAsString()` is a helper to extract string values from ini sections with defaults.\n\n**Environment Variable Override Logic** (`pkg/setting/setting.go`):\n- `applyEnvVariableOverrides()` iterates all sections and keys of the merged INI file. For each key, it computes the env var name via `EnvKey()` (e.g., section `database`, key `password` becomes `GF_DATABASE_PASSWORD`) and, if that env var is set, overwrites the key value.\n- `EnvKey()` computes the canonical `GF_<SECTION>_<KEY>` environment variable name.\n- `SectionWithEnvOverrides()` / `DynamicSection` provides a runtime-override wrapper for sections that re-reads env vars live.\n\n**Command-Line Overrides** (`pkg/setting/setting.go`):\n- `applyCommandLineDefaultProperties()` applies `default.<section>.<key>` CLI properties to the defaults file before user config is merged.\n- `applyCommandLineProperties()` applies `<section>.<key>` CLI properties after user config and env overrides.\n\n**Config Value Expansion** (`pkg/setting/expanders.go`):\n- `expandConfig()` iterates all sections/keys and runs each registered `Expander` over values, supporting `${}` (env var interpolation) and `$__file{}` (file contents) syntax.\n- `envExpander` resolves `${VAR}` patterns from environment variables.\n- `fileExpander` resolves `$__file{/path}` patterns from file contents.\n\n**Default Values** (`conf/defaults.ini`):\n- The INI file containing all default configuration values per section, loaded as the base before any user config or env override is applied.\n\n**Sensitive Config Value Masking** (`pkg/setting/setting.go`, `pkg/setting/provider.go`, `pkg/api/admin.go`):\n- `RedactedValue()` (`setting.go`) checks if a key name matches sensitive patterns (PASSWORD, SECRET, PRIVATE_KEY, TOKEN, CLIENT_SECRET, etc.) using regex, returning `*********` for matches. It also calls `RedactedURL()` to strip credentials from URL values.\n- `RedactedURL()` uses `url.Parse()` and `url.Redacted()` to strip passwords from connection URLs.\n- `OSSImpl.Current()` (`provider.go`) calls `RedactedValue()` for every key when producing the `SettingsBag` snapshot returned to the API layer.\n- `AdminGetSettings()` and `getAuthorizedSettings()` (`api/admin.go`) serve the `/api/admin/settings` endpoint, returning the already-redacted `SettingsBag` filtered by RBAC access control permissions."
}

  "text": "The Grafana configuration loading system is implemented across several key files:\n\n**INI File Parsing** (`pkg/setting/setting.go`):\n- `loadConfiguration()` orchestrates the full config load: it reads `conf/defaults.ini` as the master file, then layers overrides.\n- `loadSpecifiedConfigFile()` loads a user-specified INI config file (e.g., `conf/custom.ini`) and merges its sections/keys into the master defaults file.\n- `NewCfgFromBytes()` and `NewCfgFromINIFile()` provide specialized constructors to create a `Cfg` from raw bytes or an already-parsed `ini.File`.\n- `parseINIFile()` takes the merged `ini.File` and populates all fields of the `Cfg` struct, calling section-specific read helpers like `readServerSettings()`, `readSecuritySettings()`, `readAuthSettings()`, etc.\n- `valueAsString()` is a helper to extract string values from ini sections with defaults.\n\n**Environment Variable Override Logic** (`pkg/setting/setting.go`):\n- `applyEnvVariableOverrides()` iterates all sections and keys of the merged INI file. For each key, it computes the env var name via `EnvKey()` (e.g., section `database`, key `password` becomes `GF_DATABASE_PASSWORD`) and, if that env var is set, overwrites the key value.\n- `EnvKey()` computes the canonical `GF_<SECTION>_<KEY>` environment variable name.\n- `SectionWithEnvOverrides()` / `DynamicSection` provides a runtime-override wrapper for sections that re-reads env vars live.\n\n**Command-Line Overrides** (`pkg/setting/setting.go`):\n- `applyCommandLineDefaultProperties()` applies `default.<section>.<key>` CLI properties to the defaults file before user config is merged.\n- `applyCommandLineProperties()` applies `<section>.<key>` CLI properties after user config and env overrides.\n\n**Config Value Expansion** (`pkg/setting/expanders.go`):\n- `expandConfig()` iterates all sections/keys and runs each registered `Expander` over values, supporting `${}` (env var interpolation) and `$__file{}` (file contents) syntax.\n- `envExpander` resolves `${VAR}` patterns from environment variables.\n- `fileExpander` resolves `$__file{/path}` patterns from file contents.\n\n**Default Values** (`conf/defaults.ini`):\n- The INI file containing all default configuration values per section, loaded as the base before any user config or env override is applied.\n\n**Sensitive Config Value Masking** (`pkg/setting/setting.go`, `pkg/setting/provider.go`, `pkg/api/admin.go`):\n- `RedactedValue()` (`setting.go`) checks if a key name matches sensitive patterns (PASSWORD, SECRET, PRIVATE_KEY, TOKEN, CLIENT_SECRET, etc.) using regex, returning `*********` for matches. It also calls `RedactedURL()` to strip credentials from URL values.\n- `RedactedURL()` uses `url.Parse()` and `url.Redacted()` to strip passwords from connection URLs.\n- `OSSImpl.Current()` (`provider.go`) calls `RedactedValue()` for every key when producing the `SettingsBag` snapshot returned to the API layer.\n- `AdminGetSettings()` and `getAuthorizedSettings()` (`api/admin.go`) serve the `/api/admin/settings` endpoint, returning the already-redacted `SettingsBag` filtered by RBAC access control permissions."
}
